name: restore
description: The dotnet restore command uses NuGet to restore dependencies as well as project-specific tools that are specified in the project file

args.name: root
args.description: Optional path to the project file to restore

options.name: --configfile
options.description: The NuGet configuration file (nuget.config) to use. If specified, only the settings from this file will be used. If not specified, the hierarchy of configuration files from the current directory will be used

options.name: --disable-parallel
options.description: Disables restoring multiple projects in parallel

options.name: --force
options.description: Forces all dependencies to be resolved even if the last restore was successful. Specifying this flag is the same as deleting the project.assets.json file

options.name: -force-evaluate
options.description: Forces restore to reevaluate all dependencies even if a lock file already exists

options.name: --ignore-failed-sources
options.description: Only warn about failed sources if there are packages meeting the version requirement

options.name: --interactive
options.description: Allows the command to stop and wait for user input or action. For example, to complete authentication

options.name: --lock-file-path
options.description: Output location where project lock file is written. By default, this is PROJECT_ROOTpackages.lock.json

options.name: --locked-mode
options.description: Don't allow updating project lock file

options.name: --no-cache
options.description: Specifies to not cache HTTP requests

options.name: --no-dependencies
options.description: When restoring a project with project-to-project (P2P) references, restores the root project and not the references

options.name: --packages
options.description: Specifies the directory for restored packages

name: -r
--runtime
options.description: Specifies a runtime for the package restore. This is used to restore packages for runtimes not explicitly listed in the <RuntimeIdentifiers> tag in the .csproj file

name: -s
--source
options.description: Specifies the URI of the NuGet package source to use during the restore operation. This setting overrides all of the sources specified in the nuget.config files. Multiple sources can be provided by specifying this option multiple times

options.name: --use-lock-file
options.description: Enables project lock file to be generated and used with restore

name: -v
--verbosity
options.description: Sets the verbosity level of the command. Allowed values are q[uiet], m[inimal], n[ormal], d[etailed], and diag[nostic]. The default is minimal. For more information, see LoggerVerbosity
